gloss.en: a living thing that grows in the earth

words.example: All plants need light and water.

gloss.en: made of plastic

words.example: She put the fruit in a plastic bag.

gloss.en: a flat dish you put food on

words.example: He put some sandwiches on a plate.

gloss.en: full of fun; liking to play

words.example: The playful puppy ran around the garden.

gloss.en: an outdoor area where children play

words.example: The children are playing in the playground.

gloss.en: polite word used when asking for something

words.example: Please sit down.

gloss.en: feeling happy about something

words.example: I am so pleased to see you.

gloss.en: giving pleasure; nice to see or hear

words.example: The garden had a pleasing view.

gloss.en: the form of a word for more than one

words.example: The plural of 'child' is 'children'.

gloss.en: a small bag sewn into clothes for carrying things

words.example: He put the keys in his pocket.

gloss.en: the people whose job is to enforce the law

gloss.en: a female police officer

words.example: A policewoman stopped the car.

gloss.en: having good manners and respect for others

words.example: Please be polite to our guests.

gloss.en: to make air, water or land dirty

words.example: Air pollution is bad for our health.

gloss.en: liked or enjoyed by many people

words.example: This beach is a popular place for tourists.

gloss.en: all the people who live in an area

gloss.en: that can be done or achieved

gloss.en: to say what will happen in the future

gloss.en: to like one thing better than another

gloss.en: to make something or someone ready

words.example: She helped prepare dinner.

gloss.en: to stop something from happening

gloss.en: a machine that prints text on paper

words.example: I bought a new colour printer.

gloss.en: a professional, especially in sport

words.example: He plays tennis like a pro.

gloss.en: used to say something is likely

words.example: You are probably right.

gloss.en: a thing that is made or grown to sell

words.example: This shop sells many food products.

gloss.en: a statement that you will surely do something

gloss.en: to make the sound of a word

gloss.en: to give something for someone to use

gloss.en: to produce a book or magazine to sell

gloss.en: marks like commas used in writing

gloss.en: the reason or aim of something

gloss.en: the female ruler of a country

gloss.en: a sentence that asks for information

gloss.en: done with speed; fast

gloss.en: in a way that makes little noise

gloss.en: to some degree; fairly

gloss.en: a game or test with questions

gloss.en: a coat you wear to stay dry in rain

gloss.en: to lift or move something higher

gloss.en: a music style with spoken rhyming words

gloss.en: fairly; to some degree

gloss.en: to understand or become aware of something

words.example: I didn't realise it was so late.

gloss.en: to understand or become aware of something

words.example: I didn't realize you were so unhappy.

gloss.en: to get something that is sent to you

gloss.en: not long ago

gloss.en: a person who greets visitors at an office

gloss.en: to treat used things so they can be used again

gloss.en: to send someone to another for help

gloss.en: a machine that keeps food cold

gloss.en: happening with the same time between

gloss.en: to rest and stop feeling worried

gloss.en: to put something new in place of another

gloss.en: an answer in speech or writing

gloss.en: a written or spoken account of an event

gloss.en: careful study to find new facts

gloss.en: a spoken or written answer

words.example: I am still waiting for a response.

gloss.en: the act of coming back to a place

gloss.en: a report giving an opinion about something

gloss.en: not polite; showing no respect
